title: Nevele

[Sint-Martens-Leerne](/wiki/Sint-Martens-Leerne "Sint-Martens-Leerne") (Deinze) * d. [Bachte-Maria-Leerne](/wiki/Bachte-Maria-Leerne "Bachte-Maria-Leerne") (Deinze) * e. This [East Flanders](/wiki/East_Flanders "East Flanders") location article is a [stub](/wiki/Wikipedia:Stub "Wikipedia:Stub"). You can help Wikipedia by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Nevele&action=edit).

(Dutch pronunciation: [[ˈneːvələ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Dutch "Help:IPA/Dutch")) is a village and former [municipality](/wiki/Municipalities_of_Belgium "Municipalities of Belgium") located in the [Belgian](/wiki/Belgium "Belgium") province of [East Flanders](/wiki/East_Flanders "East Flanders"). The municipality comprises the towns of [Hansbeke](/wiki/Hansbeke "Hansbeke"), [Landegem](/wiki/Landegem "Landegem"), [Merendree](/wiki/Merendree "Merendree"), Nevele proper, [Poesele](/w/index.php?title=Poesele&action=edit&redlink=1 "Poesele (page does not exist)") and [Vosselare](/wiki/Vosselare "Vosselare"). In 2018, the municipality of Nevele had a total population of 12,179. The total area is 51.89 km2.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

[""Wettelijke Bevolking per gemeente op 1 januari 2018""](https://statbel.fgov.be/sites/default/files/files/documents/bevolking/5.1%20Structuur%20van%20de%20bevolking/Wettelijke_bevolking_per_gemeente_2011_2017.xls). *StatBel*. Retrieved 9 March 2019.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

This article is about the town in East Flanders, Belgium. For the Catskills resort, see [Nevele Grand Hotel](/wiki/Nevele_Grand_Hotel "Nevele Grand Hotel").

**Nevele** (Dutch pronunciation: [[ˈneːvələ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Dutch "Help:IPA/Dutch")) is a village and former [municipality](/wiki/Municipalities_of_Belgium "Municipalities of Belgium") located in the [Belgian](/wiki/Belgium "Belgium") province of [East Flanders](/wiki/East_Flanders "East Flanders"). The municipality comprises the towns of [Hansbeke](/wiki/Hansbeke "Hansbeke"), [Landegem](/wiki/Landegem "Landegem"), [Merendree](/wiki/Merendree "Merendree"), Nevele proper, [Poesele](/w/index.php?title=Poesele&action=edit&redlink=1 "Poesele (page does not exist)") and [Vosselare](/wiki/Vosselare "Vosselare"). In 2018, the municipality of Nevele had a total population of 12,179. The total area is 51.89 km2.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

Effective 1 January 2019, the municipality was merged into [Deinze](/wiki/Deinze "Deinze").

## Subdivisions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nevele&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Subdivisions")]

Nevele consisted of six *[deelgemeenten](/wiki/Deelgemeente "Deelgemeente")* (sub-municipalities).

| V | [Poesele](/w/index.php?title=Poesele&action=edit&redlink=1 "Poesele (page does not exist)") | 2,52 |  |

## Famous inhabitants

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nevele&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Famous inhabitants")]

* [Cyriel Buysse](/wiki/Cyriel_Buysse "Cyriel Buysse"), novelist
* [Renaat de Rudder](/w/index.php?title=Renaat_de_Rudder&action=edit&redlink=1 "Renaat de Rudder (page does not exist)"): born in [Oostakker](/wiki/Oostakker "Oostakker") in 1897, he moved with his parents to Landegem in 1909. In 1914 he volunteered for the Belgian army to fight in the first World War, where he wrote on the physical and moral pain he was suffering, until he died on 12 December 1917.
* [Rosalie Loveling](/wiki/Rosalie_Loveling "Rosalie Loveling") and [Virginie Loveling](/wiki/Virginie_Loveling "Virginie Loveling"), writers, aunts of Cyriel Buysse
* [Leo Lovaert](/w/index.php?title=Leo_Lovaert&action=edit&redlink=1 "Leo Lovaert (page does not exist)")
* [Monika van Paemel](/wiki/Monika_van_Paemel "Monika van Paemel")
